attributes.action: Ensure local emergency mental health care for children in crisis
attributes.additional_details: Children in mental health crisis are being left without safe, local care. Families are being told to travel hours in the middle of the night or are turned away due to lack of beds or county boundaries. This puts vulnerable children at serious risk. No child should be denied urgent care because of where they live. Immediate action is needed to ensure safe and accessible support for all children.
attributes.background: Provide 24/7 local mental health crisis support for children, remove barriers caused by county boundaries, and ensure children can access the nearest appropriate hospital. Increase availability of specialist beds and improve coordination between services.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign this petition which calls for similar action:
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/759345

Health policy in Wales is devolved to the Welsh Senedd. Any changes requested via the UK Government and Parliament e-petitions in relation to health policy would therefore only apply in England.

The Welsh Parliament has its own Petitions Committee. If you'd like to send your petition to the Welsh Parliament, you can find out more here: https://petitions.senedd.wales/
